statement: `Classic`—a book which people praise and don`t read.
author: Mark Twain

statement: A clear conscience is the sure sign of a bad memory.
author: Mark Twain

statement: All you need in this life is ignorance and confidence, then success is sure.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Anger is an acid that can do more harm to the vessel in which it is stored than to anything on which it is poured.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Clothes make the man. Naked people have little or no influence on society.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Courage is resistance to fear, mastery of fear—not absence of fear.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Do the right thing. It will gratify some people and astonish the rest.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Don`t part with your illusions. When they are gone you may still exist, but you have ceased to live.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Don`t wake up a woman in love. Let her dream so that she does not weep when she returns to her bitter reality.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Education: the path from cocky ignorance to miserable uncertainty.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Everyone is a moon, and has a dark side which he never shows to anybody.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Forgiveness is the fragrance that the violet sheds on the heel that has crushed it.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Give every day the chance to become the most beautiful day of your life.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Good friends, good books and a sleepy conscience: this is the ideal life.
author: Mark Twain

statement: I did not attend his funeral, but I sent a nice letter saying I approved of it.
author: Mark Twain

statement: I have never let my schooling interfere with my education.
author: Mark Twain

statement: If you don`t read the newspaper, you`re uninformed. If you read the newspaper, you`re misinformed.
author: Mark Twain

statement: If you pick up a starving dog and make him prosperous, he will not bite you. This is the principal difference between a dog and man.
author: Mark Twain

statement: If you tell the truth, you don`t have to remember anything.
author: Mark Twain

statement: It is noble to teach oneself, but still nobler to teach others—and less trouble.
author: Mark Twain

statement: It liberates the vandal to travel—you never saw a bigoted, opinionated, stubborn, narrow-minded, self-conceited, almighty mean man in your life but he had stuck in one place since he was born and thought God made the world and dyspepsia and bile for his especial comfort and satisfaction.
author: Mark Twain

statement: It`s not the size of the dog in the fight; it`s the size of the fight in the dog.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Keep away from people who try to belittle your ambitions. Small people always do that, but the really great make you feel that you, too, can become great.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Laughter without a tinge of philosophy is but a sneeze of humor. Genuine humor is replete with wisdom.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Loyalty to the nation all the time, loyalty to the government when it deserves it.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Never argue with stupid people; they will drag you down to their level and then beat you with experience.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Never put off till tomorrow what may be done the day after tomorrow just as well.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Never refuse to do a kindness unless the act would work great injury to yourself, and never refuse to take a drink—under any circumstances.
author: Mark Twain

statement: No real gentleman will tell the naked truth in the presence of ladies.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Of all the animals, man is the only one that is cruel. He is the only one that inflicts pain for the pleasure of doing it.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Substitute `damn` every time you`re inclined to write `very;` your editor will delete it and the writing will be just as it should be.
author: Mark Twain

statement: The fear of death follows from the fear of life. A man who lives fully is prepared to die at any time.
author: Mark Twain

statement: The funniest things are forbidden.
author: Mark Twain

statement: The gentle reader will never, never know what a consummate ass he can become until he goes abroad.
author: Mark Twain

statement: The man who does not read has no advantage over the man who cannot read.
author: Mark Twain

statement: The only very marked difference between the average civilized man and the average savage is that the one is gilded and the other is painted.
author: Mark Twain

statement: The secret of success is making your vocation your vacation.
author: Mark Twain

statement: The trouble is not in dying for a friend, but in finding a friend worth dying for.
author: Mark Twain

statement: The two most important days in your life are the day you are born and the day you find out why.
author: Mark Twain

statement: There are no people who are quite so vulgar as the over-refined.
author: Mark Twain

statement: To get the full value of joy you must have someone to divide it with.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Truth is stranger than fiction, but it is because fiction is obliged to stick to possibilities; truth isn`t.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Truth is the most valuable thing we have. Let us economize it.
author: Mark Twain

statement: What do we call love, hate, charity, revenge, humanity, forgiveness? Different results of the master impulse, the necessity of securing one`s self-approval.
author: Mark Twain

statement: What would men be without women? Scarce, sir…mighty scarce.
author: Mark Twain

statement: When we remember we are all mad, the mysteries disappear and life stands explained.
author: Mark Twain

statement: When you fish for love, bait with your heart, not your brain.
author: Mark Twain

statement: Whenever you find yourself on the side of the majority, it is time to reform (or pause and reflect).
author: Mark Twain

statement: Wrinkles should merely indicate where the smiles have been.
author: Mark Twain

statement: You can`t reason with your heart; it has its own laws and thumps about things which the intellect scorns.
author: Mark Twain
